analysis.reasoning: Core message: The tweet urges Guatemala’s Attorney General to prosecute corruption and not target anti-corruption prosecutors, stating the U.S. counts on Guatemala as a partner in the fight against corruption. Evaluation: US1 (honorable tradition) – Not explicitly showcasing U.S. historical values or traditions; focus is on current stance, so no. US2 (admirable society) – No mention of U.S. society, groups, or heroes; no. US3 (hostile enemies) – Does not frame an adversary against the U.S.; it critiques corruption in Guatemala’s context; no. US4 (force for good) – Yes. The U.S. positions itself as supporting anti-corruption and rule of law abroad, advocating for human rights and good governance, which fits the U.S. as a force for good. US5 (great international ally) – Partly implied by “counts on Guatemala to be a partner,” but the emphasis is on urging proper conduct rather than praising or emphasizing unwavering support; this is more conditional pressure than ally-praise, so no. US6 (at the forefront) – No claims of leadership or superiority in domains like economy/tech; no. Conclusion: The tweet promotes US4.
